title: JEAN'S PLACE - 211info
text: TRANSITION PROJECTS JEAN'S PLACE Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Provides a 60-bed short-term, transitional housing residential program. Provides case management, life and job skills training, and support services. Stays are generally 4-6 months long. Beds at a shelter are not guaranteed. 650 NW Irving Street Portland, OR 97209 Eligibility: Female-identified adults age 18 and older, with priority for those 55 and older, those with disabilities, and veterans. Abstinence from alcohol and drugs is expected of residents; random breathalyzer and urinalysis testing is conducted. Hours: Residential Program: 24 hours per day / 7 days per week Resource Center (in person or phone): Monday-Friday 8:30am-7pm, Saturday/Sunday/all holidays 8:30am-3pm; service intakes end 15 minutes before close (503) 280-4700 Get Directions Visit Website Phone/FAX Numbers Main phone (503) 280-4700 INTAKE PROCEDURE: -Visit website to submit a Residential Shelter Pre-Screening Form, call, or walk in to the Resource Center at 650 NW Irving Street, Portland, OR 97209 to sign up for a shelter bed. A team member responds to all submissions to gather more information. -Reservations only and no intake at the shelter's physical location; without a reservation, people will be turned away. Once a space is reserved, the guest may continue to use the space until they no longer need it. FEES: None DOCUMENTS: None LANGUAGES: English, interpretation by arrangement AREA SERVED: Multnomah County TRAVEL/LOCATION INFORMATION: Two blocks northwest of Union Station. On theTriMet MAX Yellow and Green lines. Services: Transitional Housing/Shelter * Homeless Women Other Services Offered At This Location: CONSUMER REGULATION - IDENTIFICATION ASSISTANCE GENERAL MEDICAL CARE - MEDICAL CLINIC HOUSING/SHELTER - ARGYLE GARDENS HOUSING/SHELTER - BARBARA MAHER APARTMENTS HOUSING/SHELTER - CLARK CENTER HOUSING/SHELTER - DOREEN'S PLACE HOUSING/SHELTER - RESOURCE CENTER TRANSPORTATION - TRIMET TICKETS If you represent this agency, update your listing here . * The community resource directory information is up to date to the best of our knowledge. However, you should always call the provider to confirm this information and make an appointment. Be sure to confirm payment information with the provider, if payment is required. 211Info does not rate, recommend or endorse any agency. We simply provide information as a public service.
